top_signals.title: Brent crude crosses $100 after tankers reportedly struck off Saudi Arabia
top_signals.summary: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
top_signals.summary: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: US Initial Jobless Claims Fall to Lowest Level Since 1969
top_signals.summary: First-time applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week to the lowest level since 1969, signaling layoffs remain muted in a stable labor market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How will Burnham govern? You asked, we answered
top_signals.summary: The FT’s northern England correspondent Jennifer Williams, columnist and Inside Politics writer Stephen Bush and UK chief political commentator Robert Shrimsley, replied to reader questions

top_signals.title: Is ABBA Voyage suitable for children? You bet it is!
top_signals.summary: It’s one of those questions that follows a person for a lifetime: what was your first gig? The Spice Girls at Wembley, since you asked – a fact at which I cringed during my goth phase but of which I am now proud. A young person’s first experience of live music is a defining moment [...]
